name: Abraham's Journey from Ur to Canaan
description: Abraham's call by God to leave his homeland in Ur of the Chaldees and journey to the Promised Land of Canaan, establishing the covenant that would shape the history of Israel.

stops.name: Ur of the Chaldees
stops.description: Abraham's birthplace and homeland, a major Sumerian city. God called Abraham to leave his country and kindred.

stops.name: Haran
stops.description: Abraham's family settled here after leaving Ur. Abraham's father Terah died here before Abraham continued the journey.

stops.name: Shechem
stops.description: First stop in Canaan where God appeared to Abraham and promised, 'To your offspring I will give this land.' Abraham built an altar here.

stops.name: Bethel
stops.description: Abraham pitched his tent between Bethel and Ai and built an altar to the Lord, calling on His name.

stops.name: Negev
stops.description: Abraham continued journeying toward the Negev, the southern desert region of Canaan.

stops.name: Egypt
stops.description: A famine drove Abraham to Egypt where he told Pharaoh that Sarah was his sister.

stops.name: Hebron
stops.description: Abraham settled at the oaks of Mamre in Hebron, built an altar, and received God's covenant promises. Later purchased the Cave of Machpelah as a burial site.

stops.name: Mount Moriah
stops.description: God tested Abraham by asking him to sacrifice Isaac. Abraham's faith was confirmed and God provided a ram.

stops.name: Beersheba
stops.description: Abraham made a covenant with Abimelech and planted a tamarisk tree, calling on the name of the Lord.

name: The Exodus from Egypt to the Promised Land
description: The epic journey of the Israelites from slavery in Egypt through the wilderness to the edge of the Promised Land, led by Moses over 40 years.

stops.name: Goshen (Rameses)
stops.description: Starting point of the Exodus. The Israelites departed from Rameses in Goshen after the tenth plague killed Egypt's firstborn.

stops.name: Succoth
stops.description: First encampment after leaving Egypt, where about 600,000 men plus women and children gathered.

stops.name: Etham
stops.description: Encampment at the edge of the wilderness where the pillar of cloud by day and fire by night first appeared.

stops.name: Red Sea Crossing
stops.description: God parted the Red Sea for Israel to cross on dry ground, then drowned Pharaoh's pursuing army.

stops.name: Marah
stops.description: The bitter waters were made sweet when Moses threw in a piece of wood as God instructed.

stops.name: Elim
stops.description: An oasis with twelve springs and seventy palm trees where Israel camped and rested.

stops.name: Wilderness of Sin
stops.description: Where God provided manna and quail to feed the people; Israel grumbled against Moses.

stops.name: Rephidim
stops.description: Water from the rock at Horeb; Israel fought and defeated the Amalekites while Moses held up his arms.

stops.name: Mount Sinai
stops.description: God gave the Ten Commandments and the Law. Israel camped here nearly a year. The golden calf incident occurred here.

stops.name: Kadesh Barnea
stops.description: Twelve spies sent into Canaan; ten gave bad reports. God condemned Israel to wander 40 years in the wilderness.

stops.name: Wilderness of Paran
stops.description: Area of extended wandering during the 40 years of wilderness punishment.

stops.name: Mount Hor
stops.description: Where Aaron died and was buried. His priestly garments were transferred to his son Eleazar.

stops.name: Plains of Moab
stops.description: Final encampment before entering the Promised Land. Moses delivered his farewell addresses (Deuteronomy). Balaam tried to curse Israel.

stops.name: Mount Nebo
stops.description: Where Moses viewed the Promised Land from the summit of Pisgah. Moses died here and God buried him.

name: David's Flight from Saul
description: David's years as a fugitive fleeing from King Saul's jealous rage, hiding in the wilderness and among Israel's enemies until Saul's death.

stops.name: Gibeah
stops.description: Saul's capital, where David served in the court and played the harp. Saul first attempted to kill David with a spear.

stops.name: Ramah
stops.description: David fled to Samuel at Ramah. When Saul sent men to capture him, the Spirit of God came upon them.

stops.name: Nob
stops.description: David came to Ahimelech the priest and received the holy bread and Goliath's sword.

stops.name: Gath
stops.description: David fled to Achish king of Gath and feigned madness to escape.

stops.name: Cave of Adullam
stops.description: David hid in this cave and about 400 discontented men gathered to him, becoming his band of warriors.

stops.name: Keilah
stops.description: David rescued the town from Philistine attack, but learned the townspeople would betray him to Saul.

stops.name: Wilderness of Ziph
stops.description: Jonathan visited David and they renewed their covenant. The Ziphites betrayed David's location to Saul.

stops.name: En Gedi
stops.description: David hid in a cave. When Saul entered the same cave, David cut off a corner of his robe but spared his life.

stops.name: Wilderness of Maon
stops.description: David narrowly escaped Saul when a Philistine raid forced Saul to break off pursuit.

stops.name: Ziklag
stops.description: Philistine city given to David by Achish. David used it as a base while Saul hunted him. Later burned by Amalekites.

stops.name: Hebron
stops.description: After Saul's death, David was anointed king of Judah at Hebron, where he reigned seven years before becoming king of all Israel.

name: Paul's First Missionary Journey
description: Paul and Barnabas' first major missionary expedition (c. AD 46-48), commissioned by the church at Antioch, taking the gospel to Cyprus and southern Asia Minor.

stops.name: Antioch of Syria
stops.description: The sending church. The Holy Spirit said, 'Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them.'

stops.name: Seleucia
stops.description: Port city near Antioch from which Paul and Barnabas sailed to Cyprus.

stops.name: Salamis
stops.description: First stop in Cyprus where they proclaimed the word of God in the Jewish synagogues with John Mark as helper.

stops.name: Paphos
stops.description: Paul confronted the sorcerer Bar-Jesus (Elymas) before the proconsul Sergius Paulus, who believed.

stops.name: Perga
stops.description: Arrived in Pamphylia. John Mark departed and returned to Jerusalem.

stops.name: Antioch of Pisidia
stops.description: Paul preached in the synagogue, declaring Jesus as the fulfillment of God's promises to Israel. Many believed but Jewish leaders drove them out.

stops.name: Iconium
stops.description: Paul and Barnabas spent considerable time speaking boldly. The city was divided; they fled when a plot to stone them was discovered.

stops.name: Lystra
stops.description: Paul healed a crippled man and was called Hermes by the crowds. Then Jews from Antioch stoned Paul and left him for dead.

stops.name: Derbe
stops.description: Paul and Barnabas preached the gospel and won a large number of disciples.

stops.name: Return through Lystra, Iconium, Antioch of Pisidia
stops.description: Returned through the cities, strengthening the disciples and appointing elders in every church.

stops.name: Attalia
stops.description: Port city from which Paul and Barnabas sailed back to Antioch of Syria.

stops.name: Antioch of Syria (Return)
stops.description: Reported to the church all that God had done and how He had opened the door of faith to the Gentiles.

name: Paul's Second Missionary Journey
description: Paul's second expedition (c. AD 49-52) with Silas, taking the gospel into Europe for the first time, establishing churches in Philippi, Thessalonica, and Corinth.

stops.name: Antioch of Syria
stops.description: Paul proposed revisiting the churches. He and Barnabas separated over John Mark; Paul chose Silas.

stops.name: Derbe and Lystra
stops.description: Paul recruited Timothy, who was well spoken of by the brethren. Timothy was circumcised because of the Jews.

stops.name: Troas
stops.description: Paul had a vision of a man from Macedonia calling, 'Come over and help us.' Luke joined the team here.

stops.name: Philippi
stops.description: First European city evangelized. Lydia was converted; Paul and Silas were imprisoned, and the jailer was saved after the earthquake.

stops.name: Thessalonica
stops.description: Paul reasoned from the Scriptures in the synagogue for three Sabbaths. Some Jews and many Greeks believed, but a mob formed.

stops.name: Berea
stops.description: The Bereans were more noble-minded, examining the Scriptures daily. Many believed, but Thessalonian agitators followed.

stops.name: Athens
stops.description: Paul preached at the Areopagus (Mars Hill), proclaiming the unknown God. Some mocked, but Dionysius and Damaris believed.

stops.name: Corinth
stops.description: Paul stayed 18 months, working with Aquila and Priscilla as tentmakers. Many Corinthians believed. Paul wrote 1 & 2 Thessalonians here.

stops.name: Ephesus
stops.description: Brief stop where Paul left Aquila and Priscilla. He reasoned in the synagogue and promised to return.

stops.name: Caesarea Maritima
stops.description: Paul landed and went up to greet the church before returning to Antioch.

stops.name: Antioch of Syria (Return)
stops.description: Paul returned to his sending church, completing the second missionary journey.

name: Paul's Third Missionary Journey
description: Paul's third expedition (c. AD 53-57), centered on a three-year ministry in Ephesus, followed by visits to Greece and a return to Jerusalem where he was arrested.

stops.name: Antioch of Syria
stops.description: Paul departed after spending some time strengthening the disciples.

stops.name: Galatia and Phrygia
stops.description: Paul traveled through the region strengthening all the disciples in the churches he had established.

stops.name: Ephesus
stops.description: Paul's longest ministry at one location — nearly three years. The word of the Lord spread throughout Asia. The silversmiths' riot occurred. He wrote 1 Corinthians here.

stops.name: Troas
stops.description: Paul came to preach the gospel. On the return, Eutychus fell from a window during Paul's long sermon and was raised.

stops.name: Philippi
stops.description: Paul visited the church and celebrated the Feast of Unleavened Bread.

stops.name: Thessalonica
stops.description: Paul passed through Macedonia, giving much encouragement to the believers.

stops.name: Corinth
stops.description: Paul spent three months in Greece. He wrote the Epistle to the Romans from Corinth.

stops.name: Assos
stops.description: Paul walked overland from Troas to Assos, where his companions picked him up by ship.

stops.name: Miletus
stops.description: Paul summoned the Ephesian elders and gave his emotional farewell address, warning of future dangers.

stops.name: Tyre
stops.description: Paul stayed seven days. Disciples urged him through the Spirit not to go to Jerusalem.

stops.name: Ptolemais
stops.description: Paul stopped for one day and greeted the brethren.

stops.name: Caesarea Maritima
stops.description: Paul stayed with Philip the evangelist. The prophet Agabus warned Paul of his coming arrest in Jerusalem.

stops.name: Jerusalem
stops.description: Paul arrived and was arrested in the Temple after being accused of bringing Greeks into the inner courts.

name: Paul's Voyage to Rome
description: Paul's perilous journey as a prisoner from Caesarea to Rome (c. AD 59-60), including shipwreck on Malta, fulfilling his desire to preach the gospel in the imperial capital.

stops.name: Caesarea Maritima
stops.description: Paul had been imprisoned here for two years. He appealed to Caesar and was sent to Rome under the centurion Julius.

stops.name: Sidon
stops.description: Julius kindly allowed Paul to visit friends and receive their care.

stops.name: Myra
stops.description: The centurion found an Alexandrian ship sailing for Italy and transferred the prisoners.

stops.name: Fair Havens, Crete
stops.description: Paul warned that the voyage would be disastrous, but the centurion followed the pilot's advice to continue.

stops.name: Storm at Sea (Euraquilo)
stops.description: A violent northeaster drove the ship for fourteen days. All hope of survival was abandoned until an angel assured Paul.

stops.name: Malta (Shipwreck)
stops.description: The ship ran aground and broke apart, but all 276 people survived. Paul was bitten by a viper but unharmed. He healed Publius' father and many others.

stops.name: Syracuse
stops.description: After three months on Malta, they sailed on an Alexandrian ship and stopped at Syracuse for three days.

stops.name: Rhegium
stops.description: The ship stopped at the southern tip of Italy before sailing north to Puteoli.

stops.name: Puteoli
stops.description: Paul found believers and stayed seven days before continuing to Rome.

stops.name: Forum of Appius
stops.description: Brothers from Rome traveled south to meet Paul. He thanked God and took courage.

stops.name: Rome
stops.description: Paul lived under house arrest for two years, preaching the kingdom of God and teaching about Jesus boldly and without hindrance.

name: Jesus' Ministry in Galilee and Judea
description: The earthly ministry of Jesus Christ from his baptism to his crucifixion and resurrection, traveling throughout Galilee, Judea, and surrounding regions.

stops.name: Bethlehem
stops.description: Jesus was born in a manger in Bethlehem, the city of David, fulfilling the prophecy of Micah.

stops.name: Egypt
stops.description: Joseph and Mary fled to Egypt with the infant Jesus to escape Herod's slaughter of the innocents.

stops.name: Nazareth
stops.description: Jesus grew up in Nazareth, working as a carpenter. He was rejected when he taught in the synagogue here.

stops.name: Jordan River (Baptism)
stops.description: Jesus was baptized by John the Baptist. The Holy Spirit descended like a dove and God's voice declared, 'This is my beloved Son.'

stops.name: Wilderness of Judea (Temptation)
stops.description: Jesus fasted 40 days and was tempted three times by Satan, overcoming each temptation with Scripture.

stops.name: Cana
stops.description: Jesus performed his first miracle, turning water into wine at a wedding feast.

stops.name: Capernaum
stops.description: Center of Jesus' Galilean ministry. He healed Peter's mother-in-law, the paralytic lowered through the roof, and called Matthew.

stops.name: Sea of Galilee
stops.description: Jesus calmed the storm, walked on water, and called his first disciples — Peter, Andrew, James, and John — from fishing here.

stops.name: Mount of Beatitudes
stops.description: Jesus delivered the Sermon on the Mount, teaching the Beatitudes, the Lord's Prayer, and the Golden Rule.

stops.name: Bethsaida
stops.description: Jesus fed the 5,000 with five loaves and two fish near Bethsaida.

stops.name: Caesarea Philippi
stops.description: Peter confessed Jesus as the Christ. Jesus revealed he must suffer, die, and rise again.

stops.name: Nain
stops.description: Jesus raised a widow's only son from the dead as the funeral procession was leaving the town.

stops.name: Sychar (Jacob's Well)
stops.description: Jesus spoke with the Samaritan woman at the well, revealing himself as the Messiah and offering living water.

stops.name: Bethany
stops.description: Jesus raised Lazarus from the dead after four days in the tomb. Home of Mary, Martha, and Lazarus.

stops.name: Jerusalem (Triumphal Entry)
stops.description: Jesus entered Jerusalem on a donkey as crowds waved palm branches and shouted 'Hosanna!'

stops.name: Gethsemane
stops.description: Jesus prayed in agony, 'Not my will, but yours be done.' He was betrayed by Judas and arrested.

stops.name: Golgotha (Crucifixion)
stops.description: Jesus was crucified at the Place of the Skull, bearing the sins of the world. He cried, 'It is finished!'

stops.name: Empty Tomb (Resurrection)
stops.description: On the third day, the tomb was found empty. The risen Jesus appeared to Mary Magdalene and the disciples.

stops.name: Emmaus
stops.description: The risen Jesus walked with two disciples on the road to Emmaus, opening the Scriptures to them.

stops.name: Mount of Olives (Ascension)
stops.description: Jesus gave the Great Commission and ascended to heaven. Angels promised he would return in the same way.
